chapter_title.en: Chapter on Barter and Transaction with Immediate Buyback (inah)

text: 4035 - وَ- رُوِيَ عَنْ بَكَّارِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ لَهُ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ الْمَالُ فَإِذَا حَلَّ قَالَ لَهُ بِعْنِي مَتَاعاً حَتَّى أَبِيعَهُ وَ أَقْضِيَكَ الَّذِي لَكَ عَلَيَّ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4035 - It is narrated from Bakkar ibn Abi Bakr, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who has a debt owed to him by another man. When the debt becomes due, the debtor says to him, "Sell me some goods so that I may sell them and repay you what I owe."
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
